title: Valid Anagram
interviewMemoryTrick: Count up for s, count down for t -all zeros means anagram

title: Valid Parentheses
interviewMemoryTrick: Push expected closing bracket, pop and compare on close

title: Valid Palindrome
interviewMemoryTrick: Two pointers from ends, skip non-alphanumeric, compare lowercase

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
interviewMemoryTrick: Expand right, shrink left on duplicate -HashSet tracks window

title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
interviewMemoryTrick: Window valid if (window_size - max_freq) <= k replacements

title: Group Anagrams
interviewMemoryTrick: Sorted string = canonical key -same sort = same group

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
interviewMemoryTrick: Expand around every center -2n-1 centers (each char + each gap)

title: Palindromic Substrings
interviewMemoryTrick: Same as longest palindromic but COUNT each expansion step

title: Encode and Decode Strings
interviewMemoryTrick: Length + '#' + string -length tells you exactly how many chars to read

title: Minimum Window Substring
interviewMemoryTrick: Expand right to SATISFY, shrink left to MINIMIZE -track have vs required

title: Generate Parentheses
interviewMemoryTrick: Two rules: open < n → add '(', close < open → add ')'

title: Longest Palindrome
interviewMemoryTrick: All pairs + at most one odd char in center

title: Find All Anagrams in a String
interviewMemoryTrick: Fixed-size window of len(p), slide and compare frequency arrays

title: String to Integer (atoi)
interviewMemoryTrick: Steps in order: SPACE → SIGN → DIGITS → CLAMP

title: Reverse Words in a String
interviewMemoryTrick: Trim, split by spaces, reverse word order, join

title: Roman to Integer
interviewMemoryTrick: If current < next → subtract, else → add

title: Longest Common Prefix
interviewMemoryTrick: Start with first word as prefix, shrink until it matches all

title: Count and Say
interviewMemoryTrick: Read previous term aloud: count consecutive same digits, say count+digit

title: Compare Version Numbers
interviewMemoryTrick: Split by '.', compare as int segment by segment, missing = 0

title: Sum of Beauty of All Substrings
interviewMemoryTrick: Beauty = max_freq - min_freq per substring. Brute force O(n²) with running frequency

title: Edit Distance
interviewMemoryTrick: Match → free diagonal. Mismatch → 1 + min(replace, delete, insert)

title: Word Break
interviewMemoryTrick: dp[i] = can reach position i? Try every j where dp[j] true AND s[j..i] in dict

title: Next Permutation
interviewMemoryTrick: Find dip from right → swap with next larger → reverse suffix

title: Restore IP Addresses
interviewMemoryTrick: Backtrack 4 segments, each 1-3 digits, value 0-255, no leading zeros

title: Reorganize String
interviewMemoryTrick: Max-heap: always place most frequent next, hold previous aside

title: Shortest Palindrome
interviewMemoryTrick: KMP on s + '#' + reverse(s) → finds longest palindrome prefix → prepend rest

title: Longest Common Subsequence
interviewMemoryTrick: Match → diagonal + 1. Mismatch → max(skip either char)

title: Isomorphic Strings
interviewMemoryTrick: Track last-seen position for both strings -must always match

title: Wildcard Matching
interviewMemoryTrick: '*' = look up (use star) OR look left (skip star). '?' = diagonal

title: Permutations
interviewMemoryTrick: Swap current with each remaining, recurse, swap back

title: Sliding Window Maximum
interviewMemoryTrick: Monotonic decreasing deque -front = max, remove expired and smaller

title: Minimum Number of Bracket Reversals
interviewMemoryTrick: Cancel matched pairs → remaining: ceil(open/2) + ceil(close/2)

title: Remove All Adjacent Duplicates in String
interviewMemoryTrick: StringBuilder as stack -push chars, pop when top matches current

title: Longest Recurring Subsequence
interviewMemoryTrick: LCS with itself, but disallow same index (i != j)

title: Count Palindromic Subsequences
interviewMemoryTrick: If endpoints match: left + right + 1. If not: left + right - overlap

title: Word Search (2D Grid)
interviewMemoryTrick: DFS from each matching cell, mark visited with '#', restore on backtrack
